query: Explain the difference between present perfect and simple past, and correct my sentence: I have seen him yesterday.
Can you fix this and tell me why: She don't likes the book, but she read it every night.
I need both rules and examples for articles and countable nouns, because I always say a advice.
Please compare active and passive voice, then rewrite: The manager approved the report yesterday.
Show me how reported speech works and change this: He said, 'I am tired and I will go home now.'
Could you explain conditionals 0, 1, and 2, and correct: If I will see him, I tell him.
I want a quick lesson on prepositions and phrasal verbs, plus fix this: She is good in maths and depends of her friends.
Teach me subject-object pronouns and also solve this: Me and him went to the market and bought us apples.
Can you mix grammar and writing advice for this paragraph? I has a dog and it is barking very loud every morning.
Explain modal verbs and polite requests, then help me change: You must to submit it tomorrow.
Please cover adjectives, adverbs, and sentence order, because this sounds wrong: She sings beautiful and quick.
I need help with relative clauses and punctuation. Correct this: The boy which won the prize, is my cousin.
Show the difference between gerunds and infinitives and fix: I enjoy to read books and swimming.
Can you explain punctuation, especially commas and apostrophes, and revise: Its raining, lets go home.
I want a step-by-step answer on direct and indirect questions, and correct: He asked where did I live?
Teach me the difference between much, many, little, and few, plus fix this: There is many water in the glass.
Please explain tense consistency in a story and fix this: Yesterday I go to school, meet my friend, and talked to the teacher.
I am confused about auxiliary verbs and negatives. Correct: She not is coming and they do not knows me.
Give me grammar rules for comparisons and also improve: This phone is more better than the other one.
Please help with noun clauses and sentence transformation; rewrite: I know that he is coming and that makes me happy.
Explain when to use will, going to, and present continuous for future, then fix: I will going to travel tomorrow.
Can you teach me articles with proper nouns and geography names? I say the Mount Everest and the France.
I need help with collocations and common mistakes, plus correct: He made a homework and did a photo.
Please explain pronoun agreement with everyone, each, and nobody, and fix: Everyone brought their book but nobody know where it is.
Teach me the passive voice with by-phrases and this sentence: The room was cleaned by her yesterday morning.
Could you give a mini-lesson on parallelism and then improve: She likes reading, to swim, and hiking.
I want to learn about articles and adjective order, because I wrote: a red beautiful big car.
Explain how to use unless, until, and while, and correct this: I will wait unless he will call me.
Please compare stative and dynamic verbs, and fix: I am knowing the answer right now.
Can you teach me discourse markers and paragraph flow, then revise: First I went home and then, because I was tired.
I need help with possessives and contractions, plus correct: The childrens toys are in its box.
Explain the difference between too, enough, and very, and fix: She is enough tall to reach it.
Can you show me how to edit for clarity and grammar? My sentence is: In the morning, the homework was done by me quickly.
Please help with question tags and intonation, and correct: You are coming tomorrow, isn't it?
I want a combined explanation of conjunctions and run-on sentences. Fix: I was late because bus missed and I ran to class.
Teach me how to use the present perfect continuous, and also correct: I have been work here since three years.
Could you explain countable vs uncountable nouns and fix this: I bought two furnitures and some breads.
Please explain determiners and demonstratives, then correct: This books are mine and those one is yours.
I need help with synonym choice and formal writing, because this sounds odd: He completed the task very fastly.
Show me the rule for bare infinitives and help me fix: She made me to laugh and let him to leave.
Can you teach me mixed conditionals and rewrite: If I studied harder, I would have passed the exam last year.
I want a grammar check plus a speaking-friendly version: Them and me is going to the library after class.
Please explain preposition errors in time expressions and fix: at Monday, in 5 pm, on the night.
Teach me how to avoid ambiguity in sentences and correct: She saw the man with binoculars while driving.
Can you combine grammar and vocabulary practice? Correct: We discussed about the issue and reached to a decision.
I need a lesson on singular they and agreement, and fix: Each student must bring their own pencil case.
Please explain the difference between say and tell, then correct: She said me the news after school.
Could you teach me how to combine short sentences, and improve this: I woke up. I was late. I missed the bus.
Explain the use of reflexive pronouns and fix: My sister and myself went shopping and bought ourselves snacks.
I want both a rule explanation and a correction for adverb placement: He always is late and never arrives on time.
Please show how to change direct speech into indirect speech and fix: He asked, 'Where you are going?'
Can you explain the difference between used to, would, and past simple, then fix: When I was a child, I would to play cricket.
I need a grammar quiz about relative pronouns, clauses, and punctuation, because this line looks wrong: The girl, who I met yesterday was kind.
Could you help me with order of adjectives and articles? I wrote: an old small wooden Indian table.
Please explain the difference between less and fewer, and correct: There are less people in the room today.
I want a combined lesson on grammar and motivation, and please fix: I don't know nothing about verbs.
Can you show me how to use if only and wish, then rewrite: I wish I am taller and I wish I can fly.
Teach me the difference between all and every, and correct: Every of the students have submitted their work.
Please help with the use of whom, whose, and who, because I am not sure: Whom book is this and who did you give it to?
I need a full answer that mixes error correction, explanation, and examples: If he will comes tomorrow, tell him I am waiting.
